objective: Make the local CleanMac app match the updated HTML design and animation direction while keeping existing app functionality intact.
exit_condition: The goal is complete when the SwiftUI app visually follows cleanmac_with_ai.html, includes the corresponding generated image assets, preserves the existing local feature flow, builds successfully, and the checkpoint plus implementation changes are committed.

phases.name: Extract HTML design and animation requirements
Generate image assets that match the HTML design
Apply the design and animations to SwiftUI views without changing core behavior
Verify the app builds and the preserved behavior still compiles

current.focus: Generated image assets are present under Sources/CleanMac/Resources, final Images PNGs are 8-bit RGBA with transparent corners, and the package build has verified they are copied into the app resource bundle.

decisions.reason: The task has a concrete completion condition: the local app should match the already-updated HTML design and animation direction.

decisions.decision: Keep local app functionality unchanged for this pass.
decisions.reason: The user explicitly asked to leave local functionality alone and handle it later.

decisions.decision: Use env CLANG_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/codex-clang-module-cache swift build as the verification command.
decisions.reason: Plain swift build tried to write toolchain cache under the home directory, and sandboxed SwiftPM manifest evaluation hit sandbox-exec restrictions.

decisions.decision: Treat cleanmac_with_ai.html as visual direction rather than a request to add every prototype page as product behavior.
decisions.reason: This phase must not change product behavior, and the existing app flow should keep the trust-first cleanup path intact.

decisions.decision: Generate a compact reusable asset set instead of copying every inline SVG from the HTML prototype.
decisions.reason: The HTML has no external image assets, and a smaller mascot plus feature-illustration set is easier to maintain in SwiftUI.

decisions.decision: Keep later SwiftUI motion state-driven and Reduce Motion aware.
decisions.reason: The HTML relies on playful looping cues, but the app should only animate active/progress/celebration states and respect accessibility settings.

decisions.decision: Use SwiftPM target resources for the app image set.
decisions.reason: Package.swift processes Sources/CleanMac/Resources for the CleanMac executable target, so the next SwiftUI phase can load packaged assets from the app bundle instead of relying on external file paths.

decisions.decision: Keep chroma-key source PNGs and ship cleaned RGBA runtime PNGs.
decisions.reason: ImagesSource preserves the generated key-color inputs for provenance, while Images contains the chroma-key-removed assets verified as RGBA with transparent corners for runtime use.
